g.text: cgroups v1 vs v2 Hierarchy Comparison
v1: Process in N cgroups (one per controller)
v2: Process in 1 cgroup (all controllers unified)
v2: Thread mode (cgroup.threads) for fine-grained control

div.p: Multiple Independent Hierarchies Each controller has its own mount point. Process can be in different cgroups per controller.
Problem: Process 1234 must be added to SEPARATE cgroups for each controller:
Single Unified Hierarchy All controllers share one tree. Process in exactly ONE cgroup.
Benefit: Process 1234 added ONCE: /sys/fs/cgroup/container_a/cgroup.procs All controllers apply automatically!
Each controller requires separate mount. No delegation possible.
subtree_control Enables controllers for children: echo "+memory +cpu +pids" > subtree_control

text.tspan: # Output: cpuset cpu io memory pids
# Output: cpuset cpu io memory pids
# User can now manage their own cgroup
# User can now manage their own cgroup

g.text: cgroup.controllers: cpuset cpu io memory pids
